meta.title: MBS Clarification Question Bank — User Flow 6

items.stem: Email scenario (software intern): Your manager replies "See me." at 4:47 PM Friday with no context. You have a family commitment at 6 PM. Best first reply:
options: Reply: "On my way now — should I bring the Q3 deck?"
Reply: "Can we do Monday 10 AM? I can share a written update tonight if urgent."
Forward to a teammate asking them to cover
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 1; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Chat: Peer DMs you "Can you just do my slide? I'm swamped." You already have your own deadline. You:
options: Ask what they tried + offer 15-min pair on structure, not full takeover
Do entire slide to avoid conflict
Screenshot and post in group chat to shame them
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 0; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Manager 1:1: They say "You're too quiet in meetings." As a marketing associate, best response in the moment:
options: Thank them, ask for one meeting where you'll prepare one question in advance
Defend that quiet people are smarter
Promise to talk more without changing behavior
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 0; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Client call: They interrupt you mid-explanation. Best move:
options: Pause, confirm their concern in one sentence, then offer to finish point or jump to theirs
Talk louder to finish your point
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 0; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Pick the response more like you when explaining a mistake to your manager:
options: Lead with facts, impact, fix, prevention
items.scoring_logic: Thurstonian-IRT ipsative; both options desirability-matched
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Peer voice note sounds flat after their idea was rejected. Best empathic opener:
options: "I can see that landed hard — want to walk through what you were aiming for?"
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 0; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Rank these feedback approaches (best → worst) after a missed deadline you caused:
items.scoring_logic: Spearman vs expert rank [0,3,1,2]; partial credit per position
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Email: A client writes "Your update is confusing and late." You delivered on time but the attachment was the wrong version. Best response:
options: Apologize for the wrong file, attach correct version, summarize changes in 3 bullets
Explain the client didn't read carefully
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 0; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Slack/Teams: Channel thread goes off-topic during incident response. You are marketing associate. You:
options: Post: "Moving troubleshooting to #inc-123 — summary every 15 min here."
Argue about who caused the bug in the main channel
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 0; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Manager gives vague praise: "Good job lately." You need clearer growth signal. You:
options: Ask: "Which deliverable helped most — and what would 'great' look like next month?"
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 0; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Client call: They interrupt you mid-explanation. Best move:
options: Pause, confirm their concern in one sentence, then offer to finish point or jump to theirs
Talk louder to finish your point
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 0; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Pick more like you when a teammate's tone feels sharp in chat:
options: Assume positive intent; ask clarifying question
Match sharp tone to show strength
items.scoring_logic: Thurstonian-IRT ipsative; both options desirability-matched
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Read-the-room: Message says "Fine. Ship it." after a long debate. Most likely intent:
items.scoring_logic: Keyed option 1; latency >8s flags faking on desirable traits
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Rank these feedback approaches (best → worst) after a missed deadline you caused:
items.scoring_logic: Spearman vs expert rank [0,3,1,2]; partial credit per position
items.anti_bias_rationale: Scenario-based or ipsative — not self-rated Likert on communication/EQ

items.stem: Email: You need to push back on an unrealistic deadline from a senior stakeholder. Tone that fits a marketing associate:
options: Data + impact + proposed alternative date with scope trade-off

items.stem: Pick more like you in team conflict: (A) Address issue directly with specific example (B) Avoid until it blows over
items.scoring_logic: Thurstonian-IRT ipsative
items.anti_bias_rationale: Workplace scenario reveals conflict/collaboration style indirectly
items.follow_up_trigger: conf(TEAM)<0.65 OR conf(CONF)<0.65 OR TEAM-COOP vs CONF-CMP divergence

items.stem: Design review: UX and eng deadlock on scope. You:
options: Map user journey; propose phased release
Ask manager to decide without context
items.scoring_logic: Thomas-Kilmann style mapping; collaborate/problem-solve weighted highest
items.anti_bias_rationale: Workplace scenario reveals conflict/collaboration style indirectly
items.follow_up_trigger: conf(TEAM)<0.65 OR conf(CONF)<0.65 OR TEAM-COOP vs CONF-CMP divergence

items.stem: On-call alert fires on your feature flag. You:
options: Acknowledge in channel, triage, rollback if needed
items.scoring_logic: Thomas-Kilmann style mapping; collaborate/problem-solve weighted highest
items.anti_bias_rationale: Workplace scenario reveals conflict/collaboration style indirectly
items.follow_up_trigger: conf(TEAM)<0.65 OR conf(CONF)<0.65 OR TEAM-COOP vs CONF-CMP divergence

items.stem: Manager credits you for fix that was pair work. You:
options: Redirect credit to pair + describe split

items.anti_bias_rationale: Workplace scenario reveals conflict/collaboration style indirectly
items.follow_up_trigger: conf(TEAM)<0.65 OR conf(CONF)<0.65 OR TEAM-COOP vs CONF-CMP divergence

items.stem: In-tray mini #1: Items — (1) angry enterprise client (2) CEO vague 'thoughts?' (3) newsletter trap (4) teammate blocker. Rank top priority:
items.scoring_logic: Weighted rank vs expert key; trap item penalty
items.anti_bias_rationale: Revealed prioritisation under pressure

items.stem: Two Doors: Launch feature with 85% test coverage vs delay 2 days for edge cases. 3 clue cards available. You typically: (variant 1)
items.scoring_logic: Telemetry: clues_opened, revisions, time_to_decide → DEC-MAX/SAT indices
items.anti_bias_rationale: Pure behavioural decision task — cannot fake maximiser/satisficer claim

items.stem: Time pressure: Customer escalation while you are sole on-call fresher.
options: Follow runbook; loop senior; customer update every 30 min
items.scoring_logic: Keyed SJT + latency
items.anti_bias_rationale: Decision under load — not self-report decisiveness

items.stem: Time pressure: Budget cut removes contractor; scope unchanged.
items.scoring_logic: Keyed SJT + latency
items.anti_bias_rationale: Decision under load — not self-report decisiveness

items.stem: Pick more like you under time pressure:
items.scoring_logic: Ipsative IRT

IN_TRAY_MINI.setup: Day-1 inbox: 3-6 items including trap (newsletter), angry client, vague exec ask, teammate blocker.

npc_responses.angry_client: Escalates if no ack in 5 min; de-escalates with timeline + owner
npc_responses.exec_vague: Rewards clarifying questions; penalizes silent acceptance

scoring_rubric.priority_order: Kendall tau vs expert [client, teammate, exec, newsletter]

TEAM_CHAT_MICRO.setup: Group chat: shortcut vs quality debate; embedded groupthink moment.

scoring_rubric.TEAM_GT: penalty if silent agreement after red flag

CONFLICT_BRANCH.setup: Missed handoff caused rework; 3-node dialogue tree.

npc_responses.peer_defensive: Escalates if accused; opens if curiosity shown

CONFLICT_BRANCH.scoring_rubric: Thomas-Kilmann mapping; collaborate/compromise highest for workplace norm

NEGOTIATION_NPC.setup: Stakeholder wants scope +2 days earlier; 2 rounds offer/counter.

npc_responses.round2: Accept trade: drop nice-to-have OR add resource

TWO_DOORS.setup: Timed dilemma; 0-3 clue cards optional before choice.

npc_responses.ops_channel: Floods with noise if no structured updates

SECTOR_MATCH.setup: Match 5 businesses to revenue models; role-weighted sector from intake.
SECTOR_MATCH.scoring_rubric: accuracy role-weighted x1.25 for target sector items

TREND_RADAR.setup: Sort 2026 forces vs decoys for target sector.

TOOL_BENCH.setup: Match tools to primary job tasks for role_target.

MARKET_MOVER.setup: 1-2 business scenarios; pricing/inventory decision moves simulated metric.

npc_responses.market: Reacts to discount depth vs margin

HONEST_DICE.setup: Report dice roll for points; system knows ground truth. 2-3 trials.
HONEST_DICE.scoring_rubric: over_report aggregate; never shown to candidate as cheat
